summary: Broad signature-correction PR across camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, item, lb, and baselib code. The author says an asm-examining Claude-assisted script produced many false positives/negatives but also legitimate signature mismatches. The diff updates function return types, removes unused parameters, reorders call arguments, synchronizes headers with implementations, and uses bool/PAD_STACK cleanup in several decomp functions. Bot report shows small text-match progress, including two new gm_1601 matches and eleven improved unmatched items.

key_files.role: Implementation signature and bool-return cleanup for Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234, plus local formatting/cast cleanup.
Header prototypes synchronized with camera.c bool return types.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234 prototypes changed to bool

key_files.role: Call sites updated after tether item helper signatures dropped an x-position argument.
Tether call signature update and stack padding cleanup in catch-related code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: UNUSED volatile u8 pad_b[8] replaced with PAD_STACK(8)

key_files.role: Call site updated after Fighter_8006CDA4 lost a third parameter.
Implementation signature for Fighter_8006CDA4 reduced from three parameters to two; bool locals and PAD_STACK used.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: void Fighter_8006CDA4(Fighter* fp, s32 arg1)

key_files.role: Header prototype synchronized with Fighter_8006CDA4 implementation.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Fighter_8006CDA4 prototype changed to two parameters

key_files.role: Call site updated after ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898 lost an unused second parameter.
Implementation signature for ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898 reduced to one parameter.
Implementation signature for ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28 changed to void parameter list because it reads Yoshi ext_attr globally.
Call argument order corrected for it_802BE9D8.
Removed temporary SOLUTION preprocessor branch and replaced manual stack array with PAD_STACK.
Game-mode implementation touched; bot report attributes two new full matches in this unit.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports fn_80168A6C and fn_80169444 newly 100%

key_files.role: Largest changed file by hunk count; exact signature changes not visible in excerpt.
Large lb implementation signature cleanup area; exact changes not visible in excerpt.
Baselib header signature cleanup area; exact changes not visible in excerpt.

what_changed: Changed 63 files with 427 additions and 515 deletions, mostly in source/header pairs across camera, fighter, game-mode, stage, item, lb, and baselib areas.
Converted Camera_8002E158 and Camera_8002E234 from s32 return types to bool in both implementation and header, and renamed local integer return flags to bool-style ret variables.

Removed apparently unused function parameters from several signatures and updated callers, including Fighter_8006CDA4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898, ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28, it_802A78B8, and it_802BAAE4 as shown in excerpted call sites.
Corrected at least one argument ordering mismatch for it_802BE9D8 in ftNs_AttackHi4_YoyoCreateItem.
Replaced some manual or temporary stack padding constructs with PAD_STACK and removed a temporary SOLUTION preprocessor branch in ftCo_ThrownMewtwo.c.
Synchronized headers with recovered function bodies for visible signature changes, including camera.h, fighter.h, ftKb_Init.h, and ftYs_SpecialN.h.
Bot report recorded +292 matched-code bytes, two new gm_1601 matches, and eleven improved unmatched items.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated both declarations and call sites together rather than changing implementations in isolation.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Fighter_8006CDA4 changed in fighter.c, fighter.h, and ftCo_Damage.c
diff_excerpt: ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898 changed in ftKb_SpecialN.c, ftKb_Init.h, and ftCo_ThrownKirby.c
diff_excerpt: Camera_8002E158/Camera_8002E234 changed in camera.c and camera.h

smart_moves.text: Bool-like return values were represented with bool return types and true/false locals where the code uses truth-state results.
The author explicitly treated the asm-inference script as noisy rather than authoritative.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script returned false negatives and false positives

decomp_lessons.lesson: Asm-driven signature audits can be useful for finding real mismatches, but PR evidence shows they require manual filtering because the author reported many false positives and false negatives.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Claude script inferred types by examining asm
human_pr_text: not perfect, false negatives and false positives, but found legitimate mismatches

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered signature changes, update the owning header and all visible call sites in the same PR slice to keep prototype surfaces truthful.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: camera.c and camera.h bool return changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not preserve unused parameters solely because an old prototype had them; if the implementation and callers support a smaller signature, remove the parameter and synchronize callers.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Fighter_8006CDA4 removes arg2 and corresponding call argument
diff_excerpt: ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28 changes from Fighter_GObj* parameter to void

decomp_lessons.lesson: Bool-returning helper behavior should be reflected in the type when evidence supports true/false return use, improving reviewability over raw s32 flags.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Camera_8002E158 return type s32->bool and ret false/true
diff_excerpt: Camera_8002E234 return type s32->bool and ret &= Camera_8002E158

naming_conventions.name: ret
naming_conventions.convention: For bool-return helper functions, local return accumulator variables may use a neutral name like ret instead of generated register-style names such as var_r6 or var_r30.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: var_r6 renamed to ret in Camera_8002E158
diff_excerpt: var_r30 renamed to ret in Camera_8002E234

naming_conventions.name: hold_item_bool
naming_conventions.convention: Existing semantic bool local naming was retained while converting integer literal initialization to false/true.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use asm-derived type/signature inference only as a candidate generator, then inspect and commit specific legitimate mismatches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script infers types by examining asm
human_pr_text: reports now mostly garbage but found legitimate mismatches

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Replace manual stack padding arrays or volatile padding with PAD_STACK where matching still needs explicit padding.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: UNUSED volatile u8 pad_b[8] -> PAD_STACK(8)
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The excerpt does not include independent objdiff proof for each PAD_STACK use; bot report only provides aggregate match improvements.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Changing function arity can improve text matching when old prototypes forced dummy arguments; bot report showed several improvements after the signature changes.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: multiple call sites drop unused arguments
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports +292 matched-code bytes and improved unmatched items
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The bot report does not attribute each improvement to a specific signature edit.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: Report for GALE01 showed matched code 63.10% (+0.01%, +292 bytes), two new matches in main/melee/gm/gm_1601, and eleven improvements in unmatched items.
No review comments or reviews were loaded in the slice.

follow_up_queries: PAD_STACK signature cleanup false positives asm script

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, merge state, counts, changed files
human_pr_text: author explanation of asm-inference script and false positives
raw_diff/diff_excerpt: representative signature and call-site changes
notes: The loaded raw diff is truncated; only representative hunks are visible, so exact edits in many of the 63 changed files are not described beyond changed-file metadata.
The author explicitly says the script was noisy; this supports a lesson about candidate generation but not blind trust in the script.
No human review comments were loaded, so reviewer intent or acceptance rationale beyond merge metadata cannot be inferred.
Bot report gives aggregate and item-level match improvements but does not prove which individual signature edit caused each improvement.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Signature recovery should synchronize body, header, and call sites
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2413 repeatedly changes function implementations, owning headers, and call sites together when correcting recovered signatures, e.g. Camera_8002E158/Camera_8002E234, Fighter_8006CDA4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F5898, and ftYs_SpecialN_GetExtAttr28.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: camera.c and camera.h bool return updates

accepted_candidate_records.title: Asm-derived signature hints need manual validation
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR body says an asm-examining Claude-assisted script found legitimate function signature mismatches, but also many false positives and false negatives; use such output as triage, not authority.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script infers types by examining asm
human_pr_text: false negatives and false positives

source_update_candidates.title: Signature-audit tools are candidate generators, not proof
source_update_candidates.text: When using scripts or AI-assisted asm analysis to infer function signatures, treat the results as candidates. Validate each candidate against implementation use, call sites, owning headers, and narrow objdiff/checkdiff evidence before committing signature changes.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: script inferred types by examining asm
human_pr_text: script returned many false positives and false negatives
text_corpus: bot report shows aggregate match improvements
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides concrete historical evidence that asm-derived signature inference can find real mismatches but is noisy enough to require manual validation.

source_update_candidates.title: Bool-like return signatures should use bool when validated
source_update_candidates.text: If a helper's return value is used only as a truth value and the implementation naturally assigns true/false, prefer a bool return type and bool local accumulator over s32 flag variables, while keeping headers and callers synchronized.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: Camera_8002E234 s32->bool, var_r30->ret and ret &= Camera_8002E158
source_update_candidates.reason: The camera changes are a clear example of type cleanup improving source truthfulness, but curator should decide whether this is already covered by existing truthful-header and conservative-naming standards.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PAD_STACK instead of ad hoc padding arrays only with match evidence
source_update_candidates.text: When stack padding is still required, prefer the project padding macro over manual dummy arrays or volatile padding, but preserve the requirement for narrow objdiff/checkdiff evidence for each use.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftCo_Attack100 replaces UNUSED volatile u8 pad_b[8] with PAD_STACK(8)
diff_excerpt: ftCo_ThrownMewtwo replaces u8 _[8] = { 0 } with PAD_STACK(8)
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR demonstrates cleanup from ad hoc padding to PAD_STACK, but the loaded slice lacks per-function objdiff proof; retain evidence requirement.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the Claude/script output as an accepted validation method; the author explicitly reports many false positives and false negatives.
Do not infer that every changed file had a signature fix; many exact hunks are not loaded because the raw diff is truncated.
Do not claim every listed bot improvement was caused by a specific visible signature edit; the report is aggregate/item-level only.
Do not infer human reviewer approval rationale; no human review comments were loaded.
